scenarios.name: Does not create duplicate memories from repeated mentions

messages.content:
You are a memory extraction system. Your job is to identify discrete, important facts
from a conversation between a user and an AI assistant that are worth remembering long-term.

Extract facts that fall into these categories:
- User preferences, habits, or personal information
- Decisions made or conclusions reached
- Important context about projects, tasks, or goals
- Relationships, names, locations, or other entities
- Technical preferences or configuration choices

Do NOT extract:
- Trivial pleasantries or greetings
- Transient information (e.g. "it's raining today")
- Information the assistant generated that isn't grounded in the user's input
- Duplicate variations of the same fact

For each fact, provide:
- "content": The fact as a clear, self-contained statement
- "title": A short 3-5 word label
- "tags": 1-3 categorization tags

Respond with a JSON array. If there is nothing worth remembering, respond with an empty array [].
Example:
[
  {"content": "User's name is Yury and he lives in Copenhagen", "title": "User identity", "tags": ["personal", "location"]},
  {"content": "User prefers Claude over GPT for coding tasks", "title": "LLM preference", "tags": ["preferences", "technical"]}
]

Respond ONLY with the JSON array, no other text.

Conversation to analyze:

User: I live in Copenhagen, Denmark.

Assistant: Copenhagen is beautiful! How long have you lived there?

- Technical preferences or configuration choices

Do NOT extract:
- Trivial pleasantries or greetings
- Transient information (e.g. "it's raining today")
- Information the assistant generated that isn't grounded in the user's input
- Duplicate variations of the same fact

For each fact, provide:
- "content": The fact as a clear, self-contained statement
- "title": A short 3-5 word label
- "tags": 1-3 categorization tags

Respond with a JSON array. If there is nothing worth remembering, respond with an empty array [].
Example:
[
  {"content": "User's name is Yury and he lives in Copenhagen", "title": "User identity", "tags": ["personal", "location"]},
  {"content": "User prefers Claude over GPT for coding tasks", "title": "LLM preference", "tags": ["preferences", "technical"]}
]

Respond ONLY with the JSON array, no other text.

Conversation to analyze:

User: I've been in Copenhagen for about 3 years now. I live near Nørrebro.

Assistant: Nørrebro is a great neighborhood! Very lively area.

extractionInputs.assistantResponse: Copenhagen is beautiful! How long have you lived there?

extractionInputs.userMessage: I've been in Copenhagen for about 3 years now. I live near Nørrebro.
extractionInputs.assistantResponse: Nørrebro is a great neighborhood! Very lively area.

scenarios.name: Contradicting information updates or replaces old memory
finalMemories: User previously preferred Python as their primary programming language but has switched to Rust due to performance requirements.

messages.content:
You are a memory extraction system. Your job is to identify discrete, important facts
from a conversation between a user and an AI assistant that are worth remembering long-term.

Extract facts that fall into these categories:
- User preferences, habits, or personal information
- Decisions made or conclusions reached
- Important context about projects, tasks, or goals
- Relationships, names, locations, or other entities
- Technical preferences or configuration choices

Do NOT extract:
- Trivial pleasantries or greetings
- Transient information (e.g. "it's raining today")
- Information the assistant generated that isn't grounded in the user's input
- Duplicate variations of the same fact

For each fact, provide:
- "content": The fact as a clear, self-contained statement
- "title": A short 3-5 word label
- "tags": 1-3 categorization tags

Respond with a JSON array. If there is nothing worth remembering, respond with an empty array [].
Example:
[
  {"content": "User's name is Yury and he lives in Copenhagen", "title": "User identity", "tags": ["personal", "location"]},
  {"content": "User prefers Claude over GPT for coding tasks", "title": "LLM preference", "tags": ["preferences", "technical"]}
]

Respond ONLY with the JSON array, no other text.

Conversation to analyze:

User: I've completely switched to Rust now. Python was too slow for what I'm building.

Assistant: Rust is excellent for performance-critical applications! The learning curve is steep but worth it.

You are a memory consolidation system. You are given a NEW FACT extracted from a conversation
and a list of EXISTING MEMORIES that are semantically similar, each with a similarity score.

Your primary goal is to AVOID DUPLICATES. When in doubt, prefer UPDATE or NOOP over ADD.

Decide what to do with the new fact:

- UPDATE: The new fact overlaps with an existing memory — same person, place, topic, or entity.
  Merge the information into a single comprehensive memory. Always prefer this when there is ANY
  topical overlap, even if the details differ. Provide the memory ID and the merged content that
  combines all details from both old and new.
- NOOP: The new fact is already fully covered by an existing memory. Do nothing.
- DELETE: The new fact directly contradicts an existing memory, making it obsolete. Provide the
  memory ID to delete. The new fact will then be added separately.
- ADD: The new fact is about a completely different topic, person, or entity than ALL existing
  memories. Only use ADD when there is truly zero overlap with any existing memory.

Important guidelines:
- Even a low similarity score (0.3+) can indicate topical overlap — look at the actual content.
- If an existing memory mentions the same person, location, project, preference, or entity as the
  new fact, use UPDATE to merge, not ADD.
- When merging (UPDATE), combine all details from both memories into one clear, comprehensive statement.

Respond with a JSON object:
{"action": "UPDATE|NOOP|DELETE|ADD", "memoryId": "id-if-UPDATE-or-DELETE", "mergedContent": "combined-content-if-UPDATE", "reason": "brief explanation"}

Respond ONLY with the JSON object, no other text.

NEW FACT:
Title: Language switch to Rust
Content: User has switched from Python to Rust due to performance requirements

EXISTING MEMORIES:
[0] ID=70f61788-f0de-4a4e-806a-0039643af039, Score=0.671, Title="Language preference", Content="User prefers Python as their primary programming language"

llmCalls.responseContent: {"action": "UPDATE", "memoryId": "70f61788-f0de-4a4e-806a-0039643af039", "mergedContent": "User previously preferred Python as their primary programming language but has switched to Rust due to performance requirements.", "reason": "The new fact directly overlaps with the existing memory about the user's programming language preference — same topic, same person, updated information."}

extractionInputs.userMessage: I've completely switched to Rust now. Python was too slow for what I'm building.
extractionInputs.assistantResponse: Rust is excellent for performance-critical applications! The learning curve is steep but worth it.

scenarios.name: Within-batch facts about same topic are merged, not duplicated
finalMemories: User lives near BIG Herlev Center and shops at multiple nearby grocery stores: REMA 1000 (very close to home), Lidl, Føtex, and Netto. User is interested in finding weekly discount campaigns from these nearby grocery stores.

Content: User shops at REMA 1000, Lidl, Føtex, and Netto near BIG Herlev Center

EXISTING MEMORIES:

llmCalls.responseContent: {"action": "UPDATE", "memoryId": "39ed2cc5-5907-4dfe-9d17-799cd9ca0a5a", "mergedContent": "User lives near BIG Herlev Center and shops at multiple nearby grocery stores: REMA 1000 (very close to home), Lidl, Føtex, and Netto", "reason": "The new fact overlaps with the existing memory about the user's location and nearby stores — same location (BIG Herlev Center) and same store (REMA 1000), with additional stores to merge in."}

scenarios.name: Updates existing memory when new related info arrives
finalMemories: User's name is Alex. He recently moved from Berlin to Munich for a new job at BMW.

extractionInputs.assistantResponse: That's a big move! Munich is a great city. How are you finding it at BMW?

scenarios.name: Extracts basic user facts from conversation
finalMemories: User's name is Alex, lives in Berlin, and works as a software engineer at a startup

User: My name is Alex and I live in Berlin. I work as a software engineer at a startup.

Assistant: Nice to meet you, Alex! Berlin is a great city for tech startups. What kind of software do you work on?

Title: User occupation
Content: User works as a software engineer at a startup

EXISTING MEMORIES:
[0] ID=f1d71fcd-5178-4693-94c0-e0719c9ffbac, Score=0.447, Title="User identity", Content="User's name is Alex and lives in Berlin"

llmCalls.responseContent: {"action": "UPDATE", "memoryId": "f1d71fcd-5178-4693-94c0-e0719c9ffbac", "mergedContent": "User's name is Alex, lives in Berlin, and works as a software engineer at a startup", "reason": "Both memories are about the same user's personal identity/profile information. Merging occupation with existing identity details."}

extractionInputs.userMessage: My name is Alex and I live in Berlin. I work as a software engineer at a startup.
